_note: Warm, natural light, rustic kitchen feel. Focus on the simple beauty of a single ingredient.

slides.prompt: A cinematic, slightly low-angle shot of a pile of golden-orange sweet potato chews on a dark, rustic wooden cutting board. The chews are perfectly dehydrated, showing a slight leathery texture and irregular, organic shapes. Warm, soft side-light creates gentle shadows and highlights the edges. A shallow depth of field keeps the focus sharp on the frontmost chews, blurring the background into a warm, inviting glow. Food photography style is natural and inviting. No text, no labels, no watermarks, no logos, no people, no dogs.

A candid, slightly overhead shot of a single, large unpeeled sweet potato resting on a well-worn kitchen counter, perhaps next to a discarded, out-of-focus bag of generic dog treats in the background. The scene suggests a moment of contemplation or a new beginning. Natural, diffused light from a nearby window illuminates the sweet potato, casting soft shadows. The counter surface is a light, slightly textured laminate or butcher block. A 50mm lens at f/2.8 creates a subtle blur in the background, emphasizing the raw ingredient. No text, no labels, no watermarks, no logos, no people, no dogs.

An immaculate overhead flat-lay shot of a single, large, unpeeled sweet potato, washed clean, positioned centrally on a smooth, dark slate surface. The composition is minimalist and clean, emphasizing the singular nature of the ingredient. Even, soft ambient light from directly above eliminates harsh shadows, showcasing the potato's natural color and texture. A wide-angle lens (e.g., 35mm) captures the entire potato clearly. Food photography style is clean and precise. No text, no labels, no watermarks, no logos, no people, no dogs.

A close-up, slightly angled shot focusing on a pair of human hands (Nalla's Dad's) carefully guiding a sharp chef's knife or mandoline to slice a raw, unpeeled sweet potato into thin, uniform 1/4 inch strips on a light wooden cutting board. The focus is on the slicing action, with a few already-sliced strips visible in the foreground. Warm, directional light from the left casts soft shadows, highlighting the precision of the cut and the vibrant orange flesh of the potato. A 50mm lens at f/2.8 creates a shallow depth of field, blurring the background. No text, no labels, no watermarks, no logos, no people, no dogs.

A warm, inviting shot of freshly baked sweet potato chews cooling on a metal wire rack, placed on a light-colored kitchen counter. The chews are arranged neatly, showing their slightly leathery, firm texture and varying shades of golden orange. Golden hour sunlight streams in from a window to the right, creating long, soft shadows and a warm glow on the chews. A 50mm lens at f/2.8 captures the scene with a gentle blur in the background, emphasizing the cooling treats. Food photography style is rustic and authentic. No text, no labels, no watermarks, no logos, no people, no dogs.

An ultra close-up, cinematic shot of several finished, perfectly dehydrated sweet potato chews stacked casually on a textured, dark gray slate plate or a dark, matte ceramic dish. The focus is incredibly sharp on the intricate, slightly wrinkled surface of the chews, revealing their golden-orange color and chewy texture. Strong, dramatic side-light from the left creates deep shadows and highlights every contour and edge, giving them a sculptural quality. A macro lens (e.g., 100mm at f/4) ensures extreme detail. Food photography style is elevated and artistic. No text, no labels, no watermarks, no logos, no people, no dogs.
